purpose: Pin the bounded interleaving model checker for the seqlock primitive — the smallest read-mostly published-value primitive in the membrane family. Production seqlock invariants (no torn read, no missed writer publication, monotone version, monotone retry diagnostic) are a strict subset of every other read-mostly primitive (RCU/EBR/left-right/BRAVO/flat-combining); proving them on a bounded model gives a structural-soundness floor any future read-mostly fast-path can rely on. NO production concurrency primitive changes are required by this bead — the manifest gates the *shape* of the model report and the precise bound the model has explored.

primitive_under_check.name: SeqlockCounter
primitive_under_check.rationale: Smallest published-value primitive whose invariants any RCU/BRAVO leaf reduces to. Choosing the smallest leverage-bearing primitive keeps the model exhaustive and the schedule space tractable.
reader_steps: decide: Stable iff ver_before == ver_after AND even

model_bounds.schedule_enumeration: exhaustive interleaving of all writer + reader steps under within-thread order preservation
model_bounds.memory_model: sequential-consistent (atomic steps are totally ordered). The model does NOT exercise relaxed/acquire-release ordering — that is the documented bound.

invariants_proved.label: Stable reads never return a value not in the writer's published prefix
Stable reads never return an odd version (writer-in-progress signal)
If the writer fully finishes BEFORE the reader starts, the reader observes Stable, not Retry
retry_count diagnostic is monotone non-decreasing across attempts

invariants_outside_bound.rationale: The model assumes SC atomic steps. A future loom/Kani lane is required to exercise acquire/release/relaxed combinations. Until that lands, production code must keep the SC orderings the model assumes.
Only 1 writer is modeled. The production seqlock has writer mutual exclusion as a precondition; multi-writer correctness reduces to that precondition holding.

The exhaustive enumerator caps at 2 writer publications (210 schedules). Larger bounds are tractable but not required for the structural floor — the algebra of the seqlock is identical at every publication count.

downstream_consumers.consumes: Any RCU/BRAVO read-mostly fast-path that reduces to a seqlock at the leaf inherits this bounded soundness floor — it must not weaken the invariants_proved set.

summary.next_safe_action: Land an opt-in loom or Kani lane that lifts the memory_model bound; until then, production read-mostly primitives MUST keep SC orderings on the version field.
